closed_reason: Turn 2 codex peer-verify reproduced C1-C5, fixed the cp949 console crash in publish.deploy.check and the non-idempotent generated_at in publish.builder.build_site, then re-ran verifier and validators to PASS.

contract_checkpoints.C1: PASS. sister main@d97514e, live d97514e (detached, read-only), remote d97514e, merge-base OK.
contract_checkpoints.C2: PASS. publish/web/{_redirects,_headers,.nojekyll} + wrangler.toml; build_site._copy_web_assets() copies all three into site/.
contract_checkpoints.C4: FAIL-then-FIXED. Rebuild idempotence was initially false because site/index.json.generated_at used datetime.now(); Turn 2 replaced it with deterministic input-derived generated_at and confirmed IDEMPOTENT_OK.
contract_checkpoints.C5: FAIL-then-FIXED. publish.deploy.check initially crashed on cp949 stdout while printing a warning; Turn 2 added console-safe output, reran preflight/verifier, and confirmed both validators PASS.

decisions: Turn 1: Deploy meta files (_redirects, _headers, .nojekyll) live in publish/web/ and the builder copies them so Cloudflare and GitHub Pages share one site/ tree.
Turn 1: X-Frame-Options is SAMEORIGIN, not DENY, so vod.html can iframe report.html on the same origin.
Turn 1: GitHub Pages workflow is workflow_dispatch-only -- no automatic deploy on push.
Turn 1: preflight treats NID_AUT/NID_SES leaks as ERROR and treats deploy-meta absence / absolute-path mention / external CDN reference as WARNING; --strict promotes warnings to exit 2 for CI use.
Turn 1: wrangler.toml is committed at the repo root because it carries no secrets, only deployment metadata.
Turn 2: publish.deploy.check must be console-safe on default Windows encodings; CLI output now falls back to replacement writes instead of crashing.
Turn 2: deploy-root idempotence is part of the scaffold contract, so site/index.json.generated_at now derives from input records instead of wall-clock time.
meta_improvements: Free hosting deploy scaffold replaces a single explanatory document with concrete Cloudflare+GH Pages control planes, a reusable preflight CLI, and a single 8-test verifier that closes regression gaps in build, structure, cookie hygiene, TOML/YAML metadata, and local serving in one run.
